audit_items.proof_trace: The codebase enforces no-UAF via five mechanisms: robj reference counting, CLIENT_PROTECTED flag deferring to freeClientAsync, server.current_client sentinel nulled by unlinkClient and checked in processCommand (L3367), replicationCacheMaster caching (not freeing) master clients on disconnect, and ACLFreeUserAndKillClients resetting c->user before freeing the user struct. The only structural gap—processInputBufferAndReplicate accessing c->reploff and c->pending_querybuf after processInputBuffer without a current_client NULL check—does not produce a UAF because the master client is cached (memory remains valid) rather than freed when its connection drops. No code path produces a dereference of actually freed memory.
